page: Microsoft Azure E16nds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Endv6 server family. It is powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

The server is equipped with 945 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. As a network-optimized and local disk-enabled configuration, it is designed to handle workloads requiring fast temporary storage and high network throughput. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16nds_v6 provides a high memory-to-vCPU density suitable for memory-intensive applications without the overhead of GPU accelerators. The inclusion of local storage and premium storage capabilities makes it a viable option for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications that demand high memory capacity and reliable storage performance.
description: Microsoft Azure E16nds_v6 is a memory-optimized and network-optimized virtual machine from the Endv6 family, powered by an Intel x86_64 processor and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include 945 GB of local disk space alongside support for Premium Storage, making it highly suitable for data-intensive operations. It does not feature any integrated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity and fast local storage without the added expense of coprocessors. The network-optimized design ensures efficient data transfer for distributed systems, making it suitable for database hosting, in-memory caching, and enterprise application workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16nds_v6 is an Endv6-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, 945 GB local storage, and Premium Storage capability for memory-intensive workloads.
